261708.explanation: We stand firmly behind our own entrepreneurs. However, choices must be made in expenditures; at this moment, we see that Noardeast-Fryslân is already perceived as very attractive and is nearing the end of its growth possibilities here and there.

261710.explanation: The FNP can agree with financial support for residents who jointly generate sustainable energy, provided this support is fully financed from national resources and does not burden the municipal budget.

261711.explanation: Existing funds for integration must be better spent and the quality better assessed.

261712.explanation: We stand for humane reception of status holders. If each municipality takes responsibility in housing status holders, building extra asylum seeker centers becomes unnecessary.

261713.explanation: This results in less waste in public spaces and makes using the recycling center more accessible and low-threshold.

261715.explanation: Agricultural land is not meant for solar panels; that can be done on roofs.

261717.explanation: Swimming in a water-rich environment is important. The FNP believes every student should be able to obtain a swimming diploma and wants to facilitate this; however, we do this without making it compulsory for schools.

261718.explanation: The FNP supports a generous sports policy and opportunities for associations to generate funds. Unfortunately, the municipality cannot sponsor every individual sports association; this will practically lead to inequality.

261719.explanation: Instead of more enforcement officers, the FNP would prefer the 'return' of the community policeman who knows the people and the area.

261720.explanation: Instead of more enforcement officers, the FNP would prefer the 'return' of the community policeman who knows the people and the area.

261721.explanation: There is now sufficient camera surveillance where needed; extra investment is unnecessary and requires significant finances. Moreover, excessive surveillance is, according to the FNP, an infringement on privacy.

261723.explanation: The FNP believes there should be sufficient social rental housing but also recognizes many people in our municipality want affordable (social) home ownership. Therefore, we support a good mix, which could also be 30% social rental housing.

261724.explanation: Fine, provided this is realized in the short term, so within 1 year.

261725.explanation: Holiday homes are for vacation. The FNP prefers regular housing. On the other hand, we believe that regular homes should be inhabited permanently as much as possible and not used as holiday homes.

261727.explanation: At this moment, this is sufficiently arranged according to the FNP. An increase brings high costs that every resident would have to share.

261728.explanation: We are pleased with our social employment and see that this is being well addressed further in our municipality. The FNP therefore prefers to focus on regular structural employment.

261731.explanation: Parking issues are not only in Dokkum but throughout the entire municipality. The FNP opposes expensive parking garages in or near the city center. However, we do support affordable options to expand and optimize existing parking spaces.

261733.explanation: We support a low-traffic city center, provided it remains accessible to people who really need to be there. This applies to people with mobility impairments, the delivery and collection of goods, and entrepreneurs and residents who need to reach their homes or businesses.

261734.explanation: The province arranges transport for this and should discuss it with the current carrier Qbuzz. The demand-responsive transport service already exists.

261736.explanation: Looking at the new plans for Kollum, there is an urgent need for more parking spaces.

261737.explanation: A motion on this has already been submitted by the FNP, but it has not been implemented. Do this in consultation with Dokwurk so that people who are disadvantaged in the labor market can participate.

261738.explanation: Accessible healthcare is an absolute priority for the FNP, and this certainly includes the Sionsberg hospital.

261739.explanation: The existing channels already serve this purpose; extra effort would require additional funds and scarce personnel.

261740.explanation: We favor as few points of contact as possible in all care cases. Not only for foster families and preferably within existing resources. This aligns with existing policy: "1 family, 1 plan, 1 coordinator."

261741.explanation: Money must be spent effectively to address problems in people’s lives, which certainly includes thorough prevention policies. Extra funding for even more activities is not necessarily the solution.

261742.explanation: Increasing the contribution to the villages and neighborhoods fund should be sufficient for this. Municipalities should facilitate rather than try to arrange everything for everyone in the municipality themselves.

261743.explanation: The personal contribution is already very low, and household help must remain feasible and affordable for the municipality.

261744.explanation: This is a much fairer principle than treating everything equally, for example if a small campsite charges a nightly rate of 15 euros and the increase is 2 euros, proportionally this is a huge increase.

261745.explanation: Earmarking every municipal income source is unfeasible and undesirable. The money should be included in the overall budget and, of course, funds can then be allocated to care and assistance.

261746.explanation: The FNP opposes multiple small-scale shelter locations in villages because this can undermine livability. If municipalities meet their targets, flow-through in asylum seeker centers improves and such locations are unnecessary.

261747.explanation: According to the FNP, holding status alone should not be a reason for priority housing allocation; we find this difficult to explain to residents who are also urgently seeking housing. Of course, there are urgency criteria that can also apply to status holders.

261748.explanation: The FNP opposes wind turbines near industrial areas, except in Hallum and Dokkum where they can contribute to local employment, provided the wind turbines are part of the smart energy hub.

261749.explanation: The FNP supports wind turbines at industrial areas in Hallum and Dokkum because they promote local employment, but only if this does not come at the expense of the landscape and living environment.

261750.explanation: The FNP opposes financial rewards for farmers solely based on reduced pesticide use, but supports initiatives for circular agriculture, nature management, and social services, where the national and provincial governments support farmers with subsidies, advice, and frameworks.

261751.explanation: We see tasks here for a strong community, not for municipal financial dependence.

261754.explanation: However, municipalities should have an emergency plan in which this is secured. It does not have to require financial resources.

261755.explanation: Adequate lighting and reflectors are important for safety. However, we also value the darkness that still exists in some parts of our municipality.

261757.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality. People must prepare themselves based on their own judgment about what is realistic and likely to happen.

261758.explanation: The municipality must work together with residents to improve home insulation to reduce energy bills. Resident initiative is also desired. Certain municipal fees can be waived.

261759.explanation: The FNP advocates for soil quality and, considering existing interest, we see no need to lower prices.

261761.explanation: The slow processing is a thorn in many people’s side. Faster processing is an important priority for the FNP. However, fewer rules should also lead to faster processing.

261762.explanation: The FNP promotes cooperation between businesses and schools by connecting parties and facilitating projects so that young people gain broad experience and see a future in our municipality, without obliging companies to create extra learning workplaces.

261764.explanation: The Frisian language is important to the FNP. Learning the language should be possible for everyone without barriers.

261708.explanation: S!N prioritizes a good business climate through infrastructure and sufficient energy network capacity before investing funds in a promotional campaign.

261710.explanation: S!N supports joint sustainable energy production; financial support from the municipality promotes this.

261711.explanation: Investing in integration equals investing in coexistence. S!N focuses on a preventive approach so newcomers feel at home, problems are prevented, and costs are saved.

261712.explanation: S!N believes the municipality must take responsibility for providing humane shelter to people fleeing war, violence, persecution, or natural disasters. The municipality also has a responsibility to other municipalities bearing a heavy burden.

261713.explanation: S!N believes it should be free wherever possible; this encourages collection and prevents littering and dumping.

261715.explanation: S!N prefers solar panels to be installed as much as possible on roofs.

261717.explanation: Noardeast-Fryslan is a water-rich municipality; it is important children learn to swim. School swimming ensures that all children can swim.

261718.explanation: For S!N, sports clubs are the heart of our villages and town. They strengthen livability and contribute to health. Therefore, S!N believes the municipality should financially support sports clubs under certain conditions.

261719.explanation: In Noardeast-Fryslân, enforcement is important for both prevention and safety.

261720.explanation: S!N wants close contacts between municipal (prevention) staff and police, but a weekly consultation hour in every village is not always necessary; this can better be handled by the neighborhood police officer.

261721.explanation: Ensuring privacy is very important to S!N. According to S!N, camera surveillance may only be used when absolutely necessary, such as at large events.

261723.explanation: We consider a higher percentage of social rental housing important so that everyone can find a home in our municipality.

261724.explanation: S!N supports housing in vacant commercial buildings, but believes the municipality should not subsidize commercial companies. We focus on cooperation in permits and repurposing.

261725.explanation: We want to protect recreation and tourism. Residents of holiday parks often live there due to difficult circumstances, so the municipality must deal with this humanely, especially given the shortage of affordable housing.

261727.explanation: This ensures that residents receive help with poverty faster. Assistance with poverty specifically prevents the worsening of problems related to finances, health, and wellbeing.

261728.explanation: Work is more than income: it provides structure, self-worth, and social contacts. Too many people are currently sidelined for too long, while in our municipality there is plenty of work left undone.

261731.explanation: S!N supports more parking spaces in the center of Dokkum, but outside the Bolwerken.

261733.explanation: Residents must be able to access their homes easily, people with disabilities need access to the center, and businesses rely on deliveries. Accessibility remains essential, even in a low-traffic city center.

261734.explanation: Your postal code should not determine whether you can participate. There are already great initiatives like ANWB Automaatje. The municipality must continue to invest money to keep facilitating this.

261736.explanation: S!N supports the plans of entrepreneurs from Kollum to create more parking spaces in the town center.

261737.explanation: A covered bike parking facility in Dokkum’s city center encourages cycling, reduces pressure on parking spaces, and keeps the center accessible and tidy. Increasingly more electric bikes are used as well, which people want to park under cover.

261738.explanation: S!N is in favor of investing in preserving the Sionsberg care facility in Dokkum to keep care accessible.

261739.explanation: Asking for help should be easy. You shouldn’t have to get lost in forms, waiting lists, and counters before someone listens. S!N stands for early detection in schools, but also in childcare.

261740.explanation: S!N believes foster families should have one fixed contact person. Money helps, but policy is crucial as well. High workloads, large caseloads, and turnover among youth care workers need attention.

261741.explanation: S!N believes in trust, connection, and the power of togetherness. People with problems tend to become isolated. Social activities precisely help to regain connection, trust, and perspective.

261742.explanation: Many elderly people want to stay active and maintain contact with others. In small villages, this is not always a given. There, the municipality can make a difference in cooperation with village interests. Loneliness must always be countered.

261743.explanation: Based on the principle of equality, the personal contribution applies to everyone. The personal contribution is already income-dependent, so people with low incomes pay less than those with higher income or more assets.

261744.explanation: This causes a lot of administration for both entrepreneurs and the municipality; fixed agreements provide clarity.

261745.explanation: It is not wise to determine and budget this already now.

261746.explanation: S!N believes that humane, safe, and well-supervised asylum reception is possible in places with sufficient facilities.

261747.explanation: Status holders are in a vulnerable, unequal position compared to other housing seekers. Furthermore, the municipality is obliged to house status holders. Without priority, this becomes an almost impossible task. Also, the effect of removing priority on the waiting list is small.

261748.explanation: Grid congestion is a big and growing problem, also for entrepreneurs. Under certain conditions, this should be possible at industrial parks.

261749.explanation: Grid congestion is a significant and growing problem. Especially when companies jointly generate and use energy, wind turbines can be part of the solution. Under conditions, this should be possible.

261750.explanation: S!N welcomes the reduction of agricultural chemicals, but we have no budget for direct financial support. The municipality should rather focus on cooperation with farmers, sharing knowledge, and bringing parties together.

261751.explanation: S!N supports maintaining facilities in villages, even if it concerns a village shop residents want to take over together. This is often a meeting place and also promotes the local economy. The municipality must support and assist residents who want to do this.

261754.explanation: The municipality should keep incidental funds available for emergencies so that action can be taken effectively in emergencies. Specific reception places do not need to be realized for this purpose.

261755.explanation: We choose safe routes for everyone, also in rural areas. With smart, energy-efficient lighting that respects people and nature. For example, LED lighting with motion sensors.

261757.explanation: S!N stands for solidarity and equality; we want everyone, including people with low incomes, to be able to prepare for emergencies.

261758.explanation: Energy poverty is a major problem. Through subsidies for home insulation measures, we help residents lower their energy bills. Our municipal energy coaches assist with this.

261759.explanation: S!N wants to create a good business climate for entrepreneurs. By applying a fair price, the municipality can ensure this. We opt for the long-term effect.

261761.explanation: We support fast service delivery in the municipality of Noardeast-Fryslân.

261762.explanation: The municipality can take on a connecting role between business and education. By making agreements, we strengthen the local economy and ties, create more opportunities for youth, and contribute to society’s future.

261764.explanation: The Frisian language is an important part of who we are. It connects people with each other and with their environment. By making Frisian accessible to everyone, we ensure that no one is excluded. This also strengthens the position of Frisian.

261708.explanation: Businesses provide jobs. Promotion strengthens the local economy. Work close to home is important. That is good for families and young people.

261710.explanation: Local initiatives deserve support. Generating energy together strengthens the community. Residents maintain control. That is bottom-up sustainability.

261711.explanation: Participation is important. Good integration prevents problems. This sometimes requires extra effort for newcomers and society.

261712.explanation: The CDA stands for solidarity. Reception must be humane. Small-scale, with support, fair distribution, and good guidance.

261713.explanation: Processing waste costs money. Making it free encourages wastefulness. The polluter pays principle remains a good principle. We will continue to encourage waste separation.

261715.explanation: Farmland is valuable. Solar panels belong mainly on roofs. The CDA wants to protect the landscape and let the agricultural sector flourish.

261717.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill. Safety comes first. Every child must learn to swim. That is important, especially in a municipality with so much water.

261718.explanation: We value club life as moments of meeting and connection. The CDA mainly supports via facilities and does not exclude occasional financial support. But the primary responsibility lies with clubs to be (financially) healthy. Self-reliance counts.

261719.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More municipal enforcement officers help with this. That provides peace and confidence in neighborhoods, villages, and the city.

261720.explanation: The neighborhood police officer is close by. A fixed consultation hour strengthens contact. Residents feel heard. And prevention works better.

261721.explanation: Camera surveillance increases safety, especially in vulnerable places like the Markt. However, it must always be used carefully and with respect for privacy.

261723.explanation: There is a great need for social rental housing for starters and families. A strong social foundation is necessary, especially in a municipality with many residents reliant on social rental housing.

261724.explanation: The market takes the lead first. Subsidies are not always necessary. The municipality must handle money carefully. So no automatic support.

261725.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended for that purpose. Permanent residence causes problems for livability and facilities. Rules must remain clear.

261727.explanation: Some people just fall outside the rules. That is unfair. More customization is needed. No one should be left behind.

261728.explanation: Work provides structure and self-esteem. Subsidized jobs help people move forward, especially during long-term unemployment. Participation is important.

261731.explanation: There are enough parking spaces in the center. We want a livable center. However, we must invest in sufficient parking spots at the park-and-ride locations. From there, you are quickly in the city.

261733.explanation: Accessibility of the city center remains important, including by car. Especially for the elderly and people with mobility issues. Good accessibility is therefore essential for shops and facilities.

261734.explanation: Not everyone is able to travel on their own. The province invests in main structures, the municipality in local solutions. This prevents isolation, strengthens livability, and ensures accessible transport for everyone. Cooperation and multi-year investments are crucial.

261736.explanation: There must be sufficient space at the central parking lot mainly. Parking facilities in the Voorstraat must be preserved. Free and sufficient. A balance is needed.

261737.explanation: Cycling deserves good facilities. Free bicycle parking encourages use. That is sustainable and good for the city center.

261738.explanation: Care must remain close by and affordable. This means an accessible and available emergency doctor service, good coverage of general practitioners, and retention of the Sionsberg medical clinic. The municipality can facilitate the retention of hospital care in Dokkum. Financing is the responsibility of health insurers and care providers.

261739.explanation: The municipality must be accessible and approachable for everyone with questions or advice. This also applies to parents and young people. That is caring policy.

261740.explanation: Fixed contact persons are important. But this can be done without extra costs. Smarter organization is needed. It is a matter of action, not money.

261741.explanation: Help must be goal-oriented. Simply more money is not always the solution. Cooperation and customization are more important. Effectiveness counts.

261742.explanation: We have vibrant villages. Many initiatives come from the villages themselves. That is valuable. The municipality facilitates. Not everything needs to be arranged from the town hall.

261743.explanation: The personal contribution keeps care affordable. However, customization is possible for those who truly cannot pay. Responsible handling of money is therefore necessary.

261744.explanation: Tourism is important for the economy. Higher taxes can deter visitors. We remain hospitable and affordable.

261745.explanation: Parking fees have a clear purpose. We do not transfer money from one area to another. Care should be financed elsewhere. Transparency is important. So, no disguised taxes.

261746.explanation: Small-scale shelter works better. Good distribution prevents nuisance. Humane, manageable, and with support.

261747.explanation: Municipalities are legally obliged to accommodate status holders, even if we do not give them priority. With the housing shortage, priority must be better balanced. Other groups also deserve priority, such as starters, care recipients, homeless people, or divorced parents.

261748.explanation: Business parks are suitable locations. Local energy generation is extremely important, especially when there are joint initiatives by companies. That is sustainable and also good for the viability of our businesses.

261749.explanation: Local energy generation is extremely important, especially when there are joint initiatives by companies. We call these energy hubs: generating energy locally, storing it locally, and collaborating smartly. For example, in Hallum and Betterwird. This is sustainable and also good for the viability of our businesses.

261750.explanation: Farmers deserve appreciation. But rewarding them is not a municipal task. National policy is leading here. We do encourage sustainability.

261751.explanation: The municipality can think along, help, and facilitate. A village shop is important for livability. But entrepreneurship mainly requires personal responsibility.

261754.explanation: In these uncertain times, emergency shelter must be immediately available. That provides safety and certainty. Being well prepared is important. This is a resilience task for the government and the municipality.

261755.explanation: Good lighting increases safety for cyclists and pedestrians, also in rural areas. This prevents traffic accidents.

261757.explanation: Some families are vulnerable. An emergency package provides security, especially in these uncertain times. That fits with caring for one another.

261758.explanation: Targeted support remains sometimes necessary. For example, during high energy costs in times of crises. But always temporary and focused. Self-reliance remains the goal.

261759.explanation: Businesses are the backbone of our society. Lower prices attract companies. That creates jobs and economic growth. Good for the region and good for employment and livability.

261761.explanation: Fast procedures provide clarity for residents and entrepreneurs. That prevents frustration. Efficient work is important.

261762.explanation: Learning-work placements give young people opportunities. Cooperation is crucial so that youngsters can learn practically in a focused way. This builds their future. Linking work and education.

261764.explanation: The Frisian language is important. But a free course is not a core task of the municipality. Initiatives already exist. Personal responsibility counts.

261710.explanation: However, the municipality can facilitate, but not structurally subsidize.

261711.explanation: The budget made available by the national government must be sufficient.

261712.explanation: According to existing national legislation: currently that is the Dispersion Act.

261713.explanation: Continue the current arrangement, which already allows for nearly free collection.

261715.explanation: The installation of solar panels must follow established policy, so according to the so-called prioritization of the "sun ladder." Hence, first fully utilize roofs and other options.

261718.explanation: It would fit well within an association fund to which the municipality contributes and where a counter-performance is also required.

261719.explanation: A larger number of enforcement officers (BOAs) does not guarantee higher safety.

261720.explanation: We prefer more focus on increased patrolling; that is better for supervision and safety. However, the police determine their own priorities.

261721.explanation: Guaranteeing privacy then becomes a problem, moreover, what issues justify camera surveillance?

261723.explanation: We applaud the ambition, but it is not realistic and could even hinder housing construction.

261724.explanation: Converting buildings is a great idea, but it must be cost-covering without subsidies from the municipality.

261725.explanation: Tolerate as long as the housing shortage lasts.

261727.explanation: National regulations on this point are leading.

261728.explanation: Long-term unemployment often has a personal cause leading to a group that we cannot place in the labor market; individual guidance is a better alternative.

261731.explanation: Park as much as possible at the peripheral parking lots around the center; these will be expanded where necessary.

261733.explanation: The inner city must remain accessible via the established traffic circulation plan for Dokkum's city center.

261734.explanation: Public transport is a provincial responsibility, and within the municipality there are already options from the Social Support Act (Wmo).

261736.explanation: Adjustments to the number of parking spaces must be made in accordance with the Kollum zoning plan.

261737.explanation: Ensure there is sufficient parking space for bicycles.

261738.explanation: This is not a municipal task due to privatized healthcare.

261739.explanation: We would like to maintain this.

261741.explanation: Investing in prevention increases clients’ well-being and ultimately reduces costs.

261742.explanation: We would like to maintain this.

261743.explanation: The Social Support Act (Wmo) is an appropriate and adequate provision. The personal contribution cannot be reimbursed through exceptional social assistance. In some cases, the personal contribution can be claimed through the supplementary insurance with the health insurer.

261744.explanation: We support a clear fee; this idea leads to an extremely complex tariff system.

261745.explanation: The municipal budget is the way to link financial resources to tasks and activities.

261746.explanation: It is a nice idea to distribute the asylum seeker reception pressure, but it leads to inefficiency. ChristenUnie focuses on one small asylum seekers' center (AZC).

261747.explanation: Of all available homes, 8% are allocated to status holders, so 92% are not. In a recently adopted motion, we established that local residents have priority in allocating that remaining 92% (within the applicable legal frameworks).

261748.explanation: Preferably linked to so-called "smart energy hubs."

261749.explanation: Preferably linked to so-called "smart energy hubs."

261750.explanation: Developments within the sector combined with national policy must lead to solutions and to less use of pesticides.

261754.explanation: It is necessary to be well prepared for emergencies.

261755.explanation: As the statement is worded, we are against it. There should be a sensible balance between safety and dark skies. Extra public lighting does not always improve traffic situations.

261758.explanation: Here we must follow national guidelines for energy compensation.

261761.explanation: Clear deadlines for processing have already been laid down.

261764.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities via Afuk to learn Frisian through various courses. Attention must also be given to parts of the municipality where Frisian is not spoken. For residents there, for example, Gronings should receive attention.

261708.explanation: Public money for business activities is only justified if new companies in Noardeast-Fryslân both contribute to the energy transition and provide sustainable employment.

261710.explanation: A bottom-up energy transition requires a municipality that adjusts rules, actively encourages, and provides financial support, so that resident collectives are not hindered but stimulated.

261711.explanation: Integration policy is not only about newcomers from afar, but also about young people settling here: those who choose our municipality should be helped to participate and stay.

261712.explanation: We stand for reception that is safe and supported, with a clear preference for small-scale facilities where refugees can participate in society from day one.

261713.explanation: Good facilities prevent waste dumping - anyone who wants residents to dispose of their waste properly must provide the right facilities for this.

261715.explanation: Solar panels should in principle be placed on roofs and business parks, but when a farmer consciously chooses to use his land for the energy transition, a careful ‘yes, unless’ policy is appropriate: with strict conditions for landscape, nature, and local social return.

261717.explanation: School swimming is not a luxury but a basic provision. In a water-rich municipality, learning to swim is essential for children's safety, and therefore the municipality must invest in this again.

261718.explanation: Sports clubs are the social heart of our villages; when they encounter difficulties due to circumstances, we believe the municipality has the responsibility to contribute financially and/or organizationally.

261719.explanation: More municipal enforcement officers in Noardeast-Fryslân are needed, especially with a preventive role towards youth: visible, approachable, and focused on safety and vulnerability - not on issuing parking tickets.

261720.explanation: We support weekly consultation hours of the neighborhood police officer in every village, because proximity ensures prevention, early signaling, and more attention to the social context and vulnerability of residents.

261721.explanation: More camera surveillance in Dokkum sounds decisive but does not automatically lead to more safety: without clear goals, capacity for supervision, and attention to the social context, it is mainly pseudo-security.

261723.explanation: With only 28% social rental housing nationally, that is not enough; in Noardeast-Fryslân we consciously choose a minimum of 35% social rental housing in new construction, so that affordable living becomes the norm, not the exception.

261724.explanation: We opt for creative and realistic housing solutions: vacant offices offer opportunities and must be able to be converted into homes with support from the municipality.

261725.explanation: We are basically open to using holiday homes as permanent residences, provided this happens within clear rules that ensure safety, livability, and spatial quality.

261727.explanation: By raising the income threshold, we offer everyone a healthy perspective and enable more people to receive timely support. This prevents problems for residents who currently fall just outside the threshold and therefore have to request more and more expensive help too late.

261728.explanation: We believe that work is healthy and meaningful; therefore the municipality should invest more in subsidized jobs so that the long-term unemployed can participate in society again.

261731.explanation: We support good accessibility and entrance to the center of Dokkum, but not at the expense of the historic inner city: additional parking spaces should be found on the edges, not in expensive and pointless prestige projects under the canals.

261733.explanation: The inner city of Dokkum must remain accessible for essential services, entrepreneurs, and the disabled, but outside of that we strive for a car-reduced city center that remains liveable and attractive.

261734.explanation: We support tailored transport between the villages, such as a demand-responsive bus. Public transport must be accessible to everyone and not depend on profit; therefore, we prefer to see this as a public task rather than a commercial service.

261736.explanation: In our view, there are sufficient parking opportunities in Kollum; it is important to make good use of the existing capacity rather than investing in new parking spaces in the center.

261737.explanation: A free, covered bicycle parking facility in the inner city of Dokkum should have been there a long time ago - this is a simple and necessary provision for an accessible and liveable city.

261738.explanation: Accessible healthcare is a social responsibility; care must be accessible for everyone, especially in a rural municipality like Noard-East Fryslan.

261739.explanation: A central point helps parents and young people quickly find the right help. Asking for help is often difficult, so this point must be easy to find and feel safe.

261740.explanation: Foster families should have one fixed contact person. This is not a luxury, but basic care. The municipality must enable this with sufficient resources.

261741.explanation: Not just "more money." Social activities can genuinely help against loneliness and relapse. We want the municipality to invest in activities that demonstrably work, are organized locally, and align well with care and welfare. So: targeted, measurable, and together with existing initiatives.

261742.explanation: Agreed that more attention should be given to activities for the elderly in the villages. The municipality does not have to organize everything itself but can take an initiating and facilitating role together with village halls, volunteers, and welfare organizations.

261743.explanation: Not everyone has to pay the same contribution, but abolishing it completely is unnecessary. However, we believe it is fair that people with higher incomes pay more than those with a low income.

261744.explanation: A fixed amount per night is the simplest and clearest. A system that fluctuates with prices leads more quickly to discussions and extra administrative burdens.

261745.explanation: Parking fees are not an end in themselves. We advocate reserving part of the proceeds specifically for care and assistance, so these funds are used visibly and responsibly for social purposes.

261746.explanation: Our preference is for small-scale shelter, spread across the villages. This is clearer and better integrated into the living environment. Shelter can only take place in consultation with the villages and with sufficient support from residents.

261747.explanation: This statement suggests a choice that the municipality does not have. The rules for housing allocation, including for status holders, are determined nationally. Our focus is not on excluding groups but on investing in sufficient affordable housing for everyone.

261748.explanation: Wind energy near industrial areas should be a core requirement. Preferably in a cooperative context, not for profit, but as a shared responsibility and concrete contribution to the energy transition.

261749.explanation: Wind turbines near industrial areas should be a core requirement. Preferably in a cooperative context, not for profit, but as a shared responsibility and concrete contribution to the energy transition.

261750.explanation: We do not believe in symptom treatment, but in structural systemic change. Therefore, we focus on supporting farmers who want to transition to future-proof operations. Beyond financial support, we offer knowledge sharing and perspective so that farmers make the right choices.

261751.explanation: A village shop is a core requirement for liveable villages. Municipal support for takeovers is therefore logical and necessary.

261754.explanation: This is an absolute necessity. In emergencies, there must be a location for residents. We must prepare for emergencies. The municipality has a duty of care to support residents in case of emergency by, for example, providing temporary shelter.

261755.explanation: Good lighting on roads and cycle paths, also in rural areas, is essential. This way residents feel safe and can safely move from village to village and vice versa in the evening.

261757.explanation: Everyone who finds themselves in an emergency should be able to help themselves. This also includes an emergency kit. The municipality must take responsibility and ensure that no one in acute distress is left to fend for themselves.

261758.explanation: In addition, causes must be investigated and solutions sought to facilitate sustainability.

261759.explanation: It's not about cheaper prices but about a fair price and a good business climate where entrepreneurs have equal opportunities.

261761.explanation: By investing in faster permit granting, housing construction starts sooner and businesses gain space to develop more quickly. This contributes to opportunities for everyone in our municipality to develop themselves.

261762.explanation: Young people deserve fair opportunities for work and development. Therefore, the municipality must work with companies to create extra learning and work placements. This is investing in equal opportunities, craftsmanship, and a strong, solidary society. Investing in youth is investing in the future.

261764.explanation: The municipality has a duty of care for the Frisian language. New residents in the municipality who want to learn Frisian should be offered a free, accessible basic course so they can understand and speak Frysk.

261708.explanation: Promotion helps attract businesses and create jobs, as long as we invest in approaches that demonstrably work and link to strong sectors and good facilities.

261710.explanation: The municipality assists citizen initiatives with permits and advice. The condition is that the generated energy and yield benefit Noardeast-Fryslân.

261711.explanation: A lot is already being done for integration. Let’s first ensure the current approach works well before investing extra money.

261712.explanation: We do not want an asylum seekers' center in Noardeast-Fryslân. If shelter is truly necessary, then only on a small scale and with municipal control: we decide where, how large, and under what conditions.

261713.explanation: Basic waste disposal should be free, but there can remain a fee for large or expensive waste streams to prevent abuse and high costs.

261715.explanation: No, agricultural land is intended for food production. Put solar panels mainly on roofs and paved surfaces.

261717.explanation: Most parents already send their child to swimming lessons before school swimming would start. For low-income families there are already schemes/funds, so extra municipal school swimming is not necessary.

261718.explanation: Associations are primarily responsible themselves. The municipality can help with advice and facilities.

261719.explanation: More enforcement officers (BOAs) have been added; we believe that is sufficient for now.

261720.explanation: Not feasible. Police capacity is limited; we prefer smart presence and accessibility by appointment.

261721.explanation: We are against permanently increasing camera surveillance in Dokkum. Camera surveillance can be used temporarily and targeted at events or specific hotspots when there is reason to do so. This must always be done with clear rules regarding privacy, transparency, and demonstrable impact on safety.

261723.explanation: To eliminate current backlogs, at least 35% of new construction must be social rental housing. This keeps housing affordable and provides residents with suitable homes more quickly.

261724.explanation: Converting offices into homes is primarily the responsibility of owners and market parties. The municipality should facilitate this with regulations and speed, but should not subsidize it.

261725.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreation, not for permanent residence. We solve the housing shortage with real homes, not by converting recreation parks.

261727.explanation: The municipality must have the authority to raise the income limit where necessary so that no one falls through the cracks. We must also ensure that existing schemes are easy to find and quickly available to those who really need them.

261728.explanation: Not more subsidized jobs, but more focus on employment agreements with employers: real jobs, with good guidance and customized solutions where needed.

261731.explanation: Yes. Additional parking spaces around the ramparts make the center more accessible for visitors and entrepreneurs.

261733.explanation: Yes. The city center must remain easily accessible by car for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors, with a good balance between accessibility and livability.

261734.explanation: This is not a municipal responsibility. However, we believe the municipality should continue discussions with the province about public transport.

261736.explanation: Yes. More parking spaces in the center of Kollum make the area more accessible and attractive for shops and visitors.

261737.explanation: Yes. A free covered bicycle parking facility makes the city center more attractive, encourages cycling, and keeps the center tidy and accessible.

261738.explanation: Yes. Healthcare in our region must remain nearby; therefore, the municipality should cooperate in preserving care services in the Sionsberg.

261739.explanation: Yes. Fixed walk-in consultation hours can lower barriers, provide quick help, and prevent problems from becoming larger.

261740.explanation: Foster families deserve one fixed contact person. This provides peace and clarity and can mainly be achieved through smarter organization without necessarily requiring extra funding.

261741.explanation: Social activities are important, but we must especially ensure that the money is used effectively. First better utilize what already exists and collaborate with associations and welfare organizations, and only invest extra if it demonstrably helps.

261742.explanation: The municipality does not need to organize activities itself. We believe in the strength of villages and neighborhoods themselves. The role of the municipality is facilitating and supporting, not executing.

261743.explanation: For residents with a low income, household help must remain affordable. The national maximum personal contribution is low and takes affordability into account. We do not support abolishing this contribution but favor customized assistance through special social assistance or individual support.

261744.explanation: We support a percentage-based charge instead of a fixed fee per person per night. This way, visitors to basic overnight accommodations pay less. This keeps vacations affordable for everyone.

261745.explanation: Parking fees are intended to cover the costs of parking (management, maintenance, and enforcement). We should not turn it into an extra tax for other purposes.

261746.explanation: We are against a large-scale asylum seekers' center and against small reception homes for asylum seekers in various villages. For small-scale reception, if the national emergency is unsustainable, we envision reception of a maximum number of people in one concentrated location with municipal coordination.

261747.explanation: Social rental housing must be fairly distributed based on waiting time and urgency, with equal opportunities for all home seekers.

261748.explanation: We support wind turbines near business parks as part of energy hubs like those in Hallum and Betterwird. They fit best there and provide locally generated sustainable energy. Without these hubs, companies and jobs are at risk, impacting livability.

261749.explanation: Wind turbines can be located where companies jointly generate and use energy: this is efficient, limits the impact on residential areas, and strengthens our local economy. Residents of our municipality may also participate in these projects.

261750.explanation: Reducing pesticides is good for nature and health, but the municipality should mainly support this through advice and cooperation. Food supply is very important to us.

261751.explanation: The municipality must assist with advice and administrative work so that village shops can continue to exist and livability in the villages remains strong.

261754.explanation: The municipality must provide fixed emergency shelter locations so residents can quickly and safely find refuge during incidents like fires or prolonged power outages.

261755.explanation: Improved lighting of roads and bike paths can enhance traffic safety. However, we must carefully consider whether this benefits our Dark Sky area.

261757.explanation: Distributing emergency packages is not the municipality’s task. We prefer to focus on good information and support, and assistance via existing schemes where necessary.

261758.explanation: Providing structural funds for energy bills is not the solution. We prefer to help with energy saving, debt assistance, and utilizing national schemes.

261759.explanation: Land prices must remain market-based, but we can offer customized solutions for companies that create jobs and invest locally.

261761.explanation: Faster permits help residents and entrepreneurs. We should invest in a more efficient organization, preferably with fixed deadlines and less waiting time.

261762.explanation: Additional internship and training places give young people job opportunities and help companies find staff. The municipality must actively make agreements with entrepreneurs about this.

261764.explanation: Frisian is important, but a free course for everyone is not necessary. We encourage targeted learning, for example via schools and residents who genuinely want to learn it, without large extra costs.

261708.explanation: Strong profiling attracts companies and jobs. This strengthens the local economy and helps maintain local amenities.

261710.explanation: Local initiatives create support and a feasible energy transition. The VVD supports this as long as it remains customized.

261711.explanation: Reception can only take place if safety, livability, and support are guaranteed.

261712.explanation: Reception can only take place if safety, livability, and support are guaranteed.

261713.explanation: Waste processing costs money. The VVD believes it is fair that the polluter pays.

261715.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and important for food production. Solar panels should primarily be placed on roofs and business parks.

261717.explanation: Swimming skills are essential for the safety of children.

261718.explanation: Sports and club life are of great importance for livability in towns and villages.

261719.explanation: Safety is a core task of the government and requires visible enforcement.

261720.explanation: Good police presence is important. But its deployment must remain flexible and effective.

261721.explanation: When this proves necessary, definitely do it. Safety is very important!!

261723.explanation: There is a need for social rental housing.

261724.explanation: Smart reuse of buildings helps reduce the housing shortage.

261725.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation and tourism, which is important for the economy.

261727.explanation: Appropriate help in the broadest sense of the word. Early action is required.

261728.explanation: The VVD focuses on regular work and cooperation with businesses and education.

261731.explanation: Good accessibility is crucial for entrepreneurs and visitors.

261733.explanation: Accessibility and livability must be balanced.

261734.explanation: This improves accessibility for the elderly and people without a car.

261736.explanation: Supports accessibility and the local economy.

261737.explanation: This results in a tidier streetscape in the city center and reduces the chance of vandalism.

261738.explanation: The Sionsberg is of great importance for healthcare and employment in the region.

261739.explanation: Low-threshold help prevents more serious problems.

261740.explanation: This increases stability and quality of care.

261741.explanation: The municipality must better inform residents about the scheme. It is important that as many people as possible participate in social activities.

261742.explanation: This prevents loneliness and strengthens social cohesion.

261743.explanation: Household help should be accessible to everyone, so that people can live at home for as long as possible.

261745.explanation: The budget must be assessed in an integrated manner.

261746.explanation: This is only possible with good distribution and support.

261748.explanation: Wind energy together with solar energy is sustainable.

261749.explanation: Logical location for sustainable energy generation.

261750.explanation: This is a task for the Province and the Water Board. Reducing agricultural chemicals is always good, but it is not the task of the municipality.

261751.explanation: Important for the livability of villages.

261755.explanation: Improves traffic safety and social safety.

261757.explanation: This is recommended and imposed by the government! We therefore see it as their task.

261758.explanation: Temporary support is needed in case of extreme costs.

261762.explanation: Connects young people to local businesses.

261764.explanation: The Frisian language is valuable. But not a core task of the municipality.

261708.explanation: The BBB wants to put the municipality on the map properly; this is good for tourism and therefore the local economy and employment. As a municipality, you can encourage this.

261710.explanation: BBB supports local energy initiatives, as long as it does not come at the expense of agricultural land and fits within the electricity grid.

261711.explanation: BBB sees integration as a duty but does not want extra local costs or rules.

261712.explanation: BBB listens to the residents of the municipality. The survey among the population was clear: our residents do not want an asylum seekers’ center in our municipality.

261713.explanation: BBB believes that the recycling center should remain easily accessible. Then there is no need to clean up rubbish outside the villages and city. Together we ensure that our environment stays clean.

261715.explanation: BBB: no solar panels on agricultural land.

261717.explanation: BBB wants to ensure swimming skills and maintain the safety net scheme.

261718.explanation: BBB supports and strengthens associations and sports facilities.

261719.explanation: BBB wants visible enforcement and safety close to home. BBB prefers more police because the police can do more.

261720.explanation: BBB wants this and preferably as accessible as possible. Let the neighborhood officer also hold office hours at schools for the youth.

261721.explanation: BBB focuses on actual safety, not "symbolic measures."

261723.explanation: BBB wants a mix of rental and ownership, but no mandatory percentages.

261724.explanation: BBB wants to encourage better use of space and transformation.

261725.explanation: BBB allows permanent residence in recreational homes under certain conditions.

261727.explanation: BBB wants customized solutions, so no strict statements about raising the income limit.

261728.explanation: BBB focuses on entrepreneurs, not on subsidized jobs.

261731.explanation: BBB wants traffic safety and good accessibility of villages, businesses, and shops. Parking cars should be possible for everyone. So minimally paid parking, preferably free. This stimulates customers for shops and businesses.

261733.explanation: BBB believes accessibility must remain easy and low-threshold.

261734.explanation: BBB wants short communication lines, local carriers, and tailored transport.

261736.explanation: BBB thinks parking and accessibility must remain easy and low-threshold. This should be addressed together based on needs. This requires customized solutions.

261737.explanation: BBB wants good accessibility; if this can be combined with, for example, solar panels, then this can be looked at together.

261738.explanation: BBB actively supports local care and facilities.

261739.explanation: BBB wants fixed walk-in spots in village halls.

261740.explanation: BBB emphasizes fixed points of contact.

261741.explanation: BBB wants to strengthen village halls, activities, and social cohesion.

261742.explanation: BBB wants to promote connection, meetings, and activities.

261743.explanation: BBB wants care to be accessible and administratively simple.

261744.explanation: BBB only wants to raise taxes if absolutely necessary. BBB does not want a standard annual tax increase, only if needed.

261745.explanation: BBB wants efficient budgeting. Caring for your fellow human cannot depend on parking revenues. Care deserves customized solutions.

261746.explanation: BBB listens to the residents of the municipality. The population survey was clear: our residents do not want an asylum seeker center or shelters in our municipality.

261747.explanation: BBB wants local ties to be given priority.

261748.explanation: BBB is against wind turbines on land (no large-scale parks).

261749.explanation: BBB is against wind turbines on land and no large-scale parks.

261750.explanation: BBB wants fair and feasible use of crop protection products. They are not poison but crop protection products. These are approved by the European Union. All to safeguard affordable food and ensure a fair income for arable farmers.

261751.explanation: BBB supports local initiatives and livability.

261754.explanation: BBB wants livability, safety, and good basic facilities.

261755.explanation: BBB wants to explicitly improve traffic safety.

261757.explanation: BBB supports vulnerable residents and the community.

261758.explanation: BBB is social and wants support where needed, without rigid rules.

261759.explanation: BBB wants to stimulate the local economy. We want to encourage business expansion at the old location, but it must not come at the expense of agricultural land (farmers must get a fair price).

261761.explanation: BBB wants fewer rules and faster procedures.

261762.explanation: BBB supports youth participation and education-work connection.

261764.explanation: BBB wants more attention for Frisian language, culture, and tradition.

261708.explanation: To keep our municipality attractive for all our residents, it is important that sufficient jobs are available.

261710.explanation: As far as we are concerned, these initiatives should be fully self-financed, without municipal intervention.

261711.explanation: We place the responsibility for integration with the newcomers themselves.

261712.explanation: We are against the establishment of an asylum seekers' center in our municipality, even if it concerns a small-scale variant.

261713.explanation: The condition is that the waste is delivered separated properly.

261715.explanation: Agricultural land is essential for our food production and should not be sacrificed for solar parks in our opinion.

261717.explanation: Schools indicate that there is no time within the timetable hours to offer swimming lessons. Therefore, we do not expect that making funds available will result in more swimming skills among our residents.

261718.explanation: A financial contribution from the municipality does not solve the causes of financial problems.

261719.explanation: For safety on the streets, this seems like a good plan to us. We are not in favor of more enforcement officers (boa's) if they are only deployed for parking policy enforcement.

261720.explanation: This takes away time from core tasks; the police need to be available at the moments that really matter.

261721.explanation: We believe that camera surveillance does not actually increase safety.

261723.explanation: There must be sufficient social rental housing for our own residents, regardless of the agreed percentage.

261724.explanation: As far as we are concerned, financing such a conversion is the responsibility of the building owner.

261725.explanation: This applies only when it concerns residents of our own municipality.

261727.explanation: We believe that the current arrangement is sufficient.

261728.explanation: Subsidized jobs often do not solve the underlying cause of unemployment.

261731.explanation: Although there is sufficient parking space for visitors within the strongholds, we advocate expanding the capacity at the park-and-ride locations.

261733.explanation: This is important for less mobile visitors, but also for residents and entrepreneurs in the city center.

261734.explanation: We consider this a form of public transport and therefore its implementation is a task for the province.

261736.explanation: An integral plan for Kollum is necessary to map future needs. Any need for additional parking capacity must be directly anchored in it.

261737.explanation: A simple, uncovered bicycle shed is sufficient to meet the demand without incurring unnecessary costs.

261738.explanation: The municipality must be an active dialogue partner for maintaining regional healthcare, even though the final decision-making authority does not lie with us.

261739.explanation: These matters can be handled well by appointment.

261740.explanation: We disagree with this. Requiring one fixed contact person makes the organization less flexible and vulnerable. In case of illness, leave, or staff turnover, support is immediately delayed if all knowledge lies with only one person.

261741.explanation: We disagree with this. Spending more money on social activities does not solve people's underlying problems and only leads to higher costs for society.

261742.explanation: Many activities for all target groups are already organized in the various community centers. We consider further expansion undesirable, partly due to the burden on volunteers.

261743.explanation: We are in favor of a personal contribution that is always determined according to ability to pay and income.

261744.explanation: Linking the tourist tax to the overnight price is practically uncontrollable because prices vary throughout the year. This leads to a huge increase in administrative burdens for entrepreneurs and high enforcement costs for the municipality.

261745.explanation: Essential care and assistance should never be made dependent on uncertain sources of income, such as parking fees.

261746.explanation: We are not in favor of sheltering asylum seekers in our municipality; this also applies to small-scale shelter spread across various population centers.

261747.explanation: To maintain livability and social cohesion in our villages, local housing seekers must be given priority in the social rental market.

261748.explanation: To protect our open landscape, we are against additional wind turbines. We only make an exception for small wind turbines (Groningen model) on private premises at (farm) businesses.

261749.explanation: To protect our open landscape, we are against additional wind turbines. We only make an exception for small wind turbines (Groningen model) on private premises at (farm) businesses.

261750.explanation: Farmers are independent entrepreneurs who know best what is good for their business operations; they do not need guidance or financial rewards from the municipality.

261751.explanation: Taking over a shop is a private entrepreneurial decision. The municipality must ensure a good business climate, but it is not the government's role to finance or facilitate takeovers with tax money. We trust in the market's and entrepreneurs' own strength.

261754.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to keep permanent and costly shelter places on standby for every possible emergency. Safety regions and emergency services already have protocols for this.

261755.explanation: We do not advocate randomly illuminating the entire countryside, but tailor-made solutions at dangerous intersections and busy cycling routes. This way we increase safety for our residents without unnecessary light pollution or waste of public money.

261757.explanation: Not a task of the municipality.

261758.explanation: Although we are sensitive to people in financial need, income support for the energy bill is a task of the national government, not the municipality. We prefer to focus on structural support through debt counseling and energy advice, instead of one-off help.

261759.explanation: Lower land prices are seen by us as an investment in the local economy, provided that the companies that settle also actually contribute to employment.

261761.explanation: Slow procedures harm the business climate; companies drop out when permit processes take unnecessarily long.

261762.explanation: Companies themselves know best how many work-learning places they can offer and what quality they can deliver. It is the task of entrepreneurs and educational institutions to coordinate this, not the municipality.

261764.explanation: The municipality must focus on its core tasks. Subsidizing language courses is not part of that, especially because there is already sufficient private supply available.

261710.explanation: Financial support must be possible in the first instance.

261711.explanation: It is of great importance that newcomers integrate into the community as quickly as possible.

261712.explanation: As long as people are fleeing from war and violence, asylum seeker centers will be necessary. D66 does believe these should be small centers with good guidance.

261713.explanation: To ensure that all waste is handed in and that there is as little litter as possible left, it is important that the costs at the recycling center are minimal.

261715.explanation: The preference is for this to be done in a way that includes participation from the agricultural sector.

261717.explanation: Sports and exercise, especially swimming, are important in a water-rich environment.

261718.explanation: Sports and exercise are important; besides financial support, it is also crucial that the club receives help to get organizational matters in order.

261719.explanation: Safety on the streets will become an important issue.

261720.explanation: Discuss the frequency with the village interests.

261721.explanation: We find it important that privacy is guaranteed. Therefore, the necessity of camera surveillance must first be demonstrated and applied restrictively if needed.

261723.explanation: The preference for new homes to be built must first be established, taking into account preferences, needs, target groups, and future household compositions.

261724.explanation: Vacancy, including office vacancy, must be countered.

261725.explanation: Holiday homes may be permanently inhabited to help address the housing shortage.

261727.explanation: The income limit is a good instrument to combat poverty.

261728.explanation: Subsidized jobs are a way to give people with a distance to the labor market a chance. It is important that this leads to a permanent employment contract.

261731.explanation: More and easily accessible parking spaces should be created on the edge of the center.

261733.explanation: If we provide good parking spaces just outside the center, we can limit traffic in the city center.

261734.explanation: Public transport must also be an option for the villages.

261736.explanation: In addition, Voorstraat must become low-traffic for the shopping public.

261737.explanation: To prevent bicycles from being parked everywhere, it is good to have a bike parking facility.

261738.explanation: The Sionsberg is important for Noardeast-Fryslân, also for the future, and must continue to be supported.

261739.explanation: If parents and young people can ask for help without barriers, assistance can begin at an early stage, reducing escalation of the problem. An additional advantage is that the care will then be much less costly.

261740.explanation: D66 is a strong supporter of solving problems in youth care. If we take Veendam as an example and implement their approach, it will not be necessary to allocate extra funds.

261741.explanation: Low-threshold activities focusing on meeting others have proven effective in helping people with problems out of isolation and should be supported.

261742.explanation: The municipality must support low-threshold activities for the elderly if these activities are supervised in cooperation with the community.

261743.explanation: At a time when poverty is becoming a bigger problem again, it is unwise to cut back on social services.

261744.explanation: A tourist tax as a percentage of the costs of an overnight stay is a good idea.

261745.explanation: Parking fees should be used to expand parking spaces.

261746.explanation: Small shelters for asylum seekers cause less nuisance and contribute to rapid integration.

261747.explanation: At this moment, it must remain possible to give certain groups priority for housing. This may change based on research into needed housing.

261748.explanation: Due to issues with energy transport, it is advisable to place energy production close to the user.

261749.explanation: Due to issues with energy transport, it is advisable to place energy production close to the user.

261750.explanation: We support compensation, but then in a comprehensive package by the national or provincial government.

261751.explanation: Community initiatives should be honored whenever possible.

261754.explanation: Permanent emergency shelters are important, where village halls and neighborhood buildings can play a key role.

261755.explanation: Safety on streets and bicycle paths is becoming increasingly important, and good lighting can be a remedy.

261757.explanation: At a time when poverty is becoming a bigger problem again, it is wise to expand social services.

261758.explanation: At a time when poverty is becoming a bigger problem again, it is unwise to cut back on social services.

261759.explanation: To increase economic activity in the region, it is good to lower the land price of an industrial estate.

261761.explanation: Permits and applications must be handled efficiently.

261762.explanation: In consultation with education and businesses, we must make agreements about jointly training future professionals. A good example could be a care campus at the Sionsberg.

261764.explanation: We consider Frisian as a language very important and believe it should be a fixed part of education. On the other hand, we believe that a Frisian course does not need to be free.

statements.title: Dropping off all types of waste at the recycling center should be free.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van alle soorten afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality must ask the police to hold a weekly meeting with the community police officer in each village.
There should be a free covered bicycle parking facility in Dokkum's city center.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The municipality should let the tourist tax rise in line with the price of accommodation.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

titleParts.text: laten meestijgen met de prijs van de overnachting.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in Kollum town center.
The municipality should help more people by raising the income threshold for qualifying for poverty assistance.
statements.moreInfo: Singles can currently receive help if they earn less than about €1,645 per month. People who live together can receive help if they earn less than about €2,294 per month combined. The lower the income, the more help someone can receive. The proposition concerns whether this income threshold should be raised so that more people can receive assistance.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Dokkum.
At least 35% of newly built homes in Noardeast-Fryslân should be social rental housing. Currently, 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Noardeast-Fryslân moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should improve lighting on roads and bike paths, including in rural areas.
The municipality should continue funding the promotional campaign to market Noardeast-Fryslân as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld blijven steken in de

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: voor Noardeast-Fryslân als aantrekkelijke vestigingsplaats voor bedrijven.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.moreInfo: According to the national Dispersion Act (Spreidingswet), every municipality must accommodate asylum seekers. Under the law Noardeast-Fryslân must accommodate about 234 asylum seekers. At the moment there are no reception places for asylum seekers in the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allow wind turbines at sites where companies jointly generate and use energy.
The municipality should help residents who want to take over the shop in their village.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should invest in permanent shelter locations where people can go in emergencies (such as fire or prolonged power outages).
The municipality should organize regular drop-in consultation hours where parents and young people can ask for help without a referral.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be deployed in Noardeast-Fryslân.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality should organize activities for older people in the villages more often.
There must be many more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.

titleParts.text: voor langdurig werklozen komen in onze gemeente.

statements.title: Status holders should not be given priority when allocating social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Statushouders mogen geen voorrang krijgen bij de toewijzing van

titleParts.glossary: Rental homes that at the start of the tenancy are not more expensive than a certain amount. In 2026 that amount is €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on social activities for people facing difficulties.
Dokkum's city center must remain easily accessible by car.
The municipality should once again provide funding for school swimming.
The municipality should not give money to low-income people to pay their energy bills.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should apply lower land prices on business parks.
The municipality should invest in a free Frisian language course for residents.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should invest in tailored transport between the local centers (for example a dial‑a‑ride bus).
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.
More parking spaces should be created in the center of Dokkum.
The municipality should invest money in preserving the Sionsberg care facility in Dokkum.
The municipality should more often allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should allocate extra funds so that foster families have one permanent contact person.
The municipality should abolish the personal contribution for low-income residents who receive domestic help.
Noardeast-Fryslân should have several small asylum seeker reception centers spread across different villages.
The municipality should invest in speeding up the processing of permit applications.
A portion of the revenue from parking fees should go to care and assistance services in the municipality.
The municipality should make agreements with companies about extra work-study placements for young people.
The municipality should allow wind turbines near industrial estates.
The municipality should financially reward farmers if they use fewer pesticides.
Low-income families should receive an emergency package from the municipality.
